---
title: "eReefs and the ereefs package"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{eReefs and the ereefs package}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
stopifnot(require(knitr))
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.path = "vignette-fig-",
  fig.asp = 0.8,
  out.width = "60%",
  fig.align = "center",
  dpi = 120,
  eval = identical(Sys.getenv("NOT_CRAN"), "true")
)
```

## What information and tools are there about eReefs?

As mentioned on this package's [README page](https://open-aims.github.io/ereefs/), eReefs is a collaborative information system created by the [Great Barrier Reef Foundation](https://www.barrierreef.org/), [CSIRO](https://www.csiro.au/), the [Australian Institute of Marine Science](https://www.aims.gov.au/), [Bureau of Meteorology](https://www.bom.gov.au/), and [Queensland Government](https://www.qld.gov.au/). As such, each collaborator has been working on different ways to visualise and explore what is currently happening on the reef, and what will likely happen in the future.

These tools and information are hosted on multiple websites. For example, while the main [website](https://www.ereefs.org.au/) combines info from all the parts:

- CSIRO has a [visualisation portal](https://portal.ereefs.info/) and an [information site](https://research.csiro.au/ereefs/);

- The National Computational Infrastructure (NCI)---Australia's leading high-performance data, storage and computing organisation---hosts [the main eReefs THREDDS catalog](https://thredds.nci.org.au/thredds/catalog/catalogs/fx3/catalog.html): the raw CSIRO model data with the highest time resolution (hourly for hydrology model, daily for biogeochemistry) and curvilinear grid geometry;

- The Bureau of Meteorology provides the [ReefTemp NextGen](http://www.bom.gov.au/environment/activities/reeftemp/reeftemp.shtml)---a set of high resolution mapping products that provide information on coral bleaching risk for the Great Barrier Reef region;

- The Australian Institute of Marine Science (AIMS) also provides a [Visualisation Portal](https://ereefs.aims.gov.au/ereefs-aims), and maintains this [R package](https://github.com/open-aims/ereefs/). In addition, the AIMS [eReefs data service](https://thredds.ereefs.aims.gov.au/) contains aggregated data services (daily, monthly, annual for hydrology model, monthly and annual for biogeochemistry) on a regular grid. The current package now supports both the original curvilinear NCI products and these regular-grid products by reconstructing plotting geometry from cell centres when corner coordinates are not supplied.

The package now resolves multi-file time windows from THREDDS catalogs directly rather than reconstructing filenames from legacy monthly stems, which makes the live OPeNDAP workflow more robust against current server layouts.

## When is this R package not as useful?

If you want to extract time-series of the most commonly needed physical and chemical variables from the main versions of the eReefs model outputs at a fixed set of geolocations at a fixed depth from mean sea level, the AIMS [web-based eReefs data extraction tool](https://extraction.ereefs.aims.gov.au/) is usually faster and easier to use. If you want animations of the most commonly-needed variables at the surface, and do not need them to be customised, the [AIMS eReefs visualisation portal](https://ereefs.aims.gov.au/ereefs-aims) offers pre-generated animations at several scales in an easy-to-navigate format.

## Other related content

- [Snippets](https://ereefs.aims.gov.au/ereefs-aims/help) of code for interactive with the eReefs data, including in [python](https://ereefs.aims.gov.au/ereefs-aims/help/how-to-plot-aims-ereefs-data-with-python).

- The [GBRF website](https://www.barrierreef.org/what-we-do/projects/eReefs), with videos and descriptions by comms professionals.

<!--
metadata:
- gpt_version: GPT-5 Codex
- time: 16:18
- date: 2026-04-26
- prompt_used: "Refactor the eReefs R toolkit away from ncdf4 toward tidync, add regular-grid support alongside curvilinear grids, archive existing R files, and refresh docs/examples."
-->
<!--
metadata:
- gpt_version: GPT-5 Codex
- time: 19:48
- date: 2026-04-26
- prompt_used: "Install dependencies, verify the refactored toolkit, improve efficiency for large THREDDS-served files, and build a working Jupyter demo notebook."
-->
<!--
metadata:
- gpt_version: GPT-5 Codex
- time: 18:18
- date: 2026-04-27
- prompt_used: "Get pandoc working, test the vignettes, and update stale vignette text to match the current animation and live-catalog behavior."
-->
